title: thymus serpyllum subsp. macrophyllus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. macrophyllus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its large leaves and small, fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of wild thyme include its antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Wild thyme is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, to promote relaxation, alleviate stress, and soothe digestive issues.
